feed.title: Howl Street — Global Financial Wire
feed.subtitle: Loudest Howls and the Pack — auto-curated from 100+ global sources.

entry.title: [LOUDEST HOWL] Trump reviews Iranian proposal aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: The White House confirms Trump met his national security advisers on Monday to discuss the plan.

entry.title: Fitch switches outlook on Landbank, DBP to ‘negative’ #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Fitch Ratings has revised the outlooks on the country’s two state-owned lenders to “negative,” following a similar sovereign credit outlook downgrade last week. This signaled pressure on the government’s ability and willingness to support the banks. In a commentary on Tuesday, the London-based debt watchdog said the government support ratings (GSR) of

entry.title: Airfares to stay high in May despite slight dip in fuel surcharge #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Air travel will continue to face elevated fares in the first half of May after the Civil Aeronautics Board (CAB) set a Level 18 fuel surcharge for the period. This was only slightly lower than the near-maximum rates imposed in late April. Under Level 18, airlines may impose fuel surcharges ranging from

entry.title: PSEi extends slump to fifth day on war, inflation concerns #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, PHILIPPINES — The Philippine Stock Exchange Index (PSEi) fell on Tuesday, extending losses to a fifth straight session. Investors stayed cautious over geopolitical tensions and rising inflation risks. The benchmark index slipped 0.58 percent or 34.36 points to close at 5,866.79. READ: Oil rises, stock markets mixed as US-Iran peace talk hopes dim Philstocks

entry.title: Peso sets new record low, closed at 61.30:$1 #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The Philippine peso finished past the 61-per-dollar level on Tuesday, marking a new record low. This, as the US dollar continues its bullish run amid uncertainties over the Middle East conflict. READ: US dollar still dominates forex markets The local currency weakened by 59 centavos to close at 61.30 against the greenback,

entry.title: SM Prime Q1 bottom line hovers at P11.7B #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — SM Prime Holdings Inc. posted a first-quarter net income of P11.66 billion this year. This was largely flat from the year-ago P11.65 billion, as higher costs offset modest revenue growth. In a disclosure on Tuesday, the Sy family-led property giant said its total revenues rose 2 percent to P33.3 billion. READ: SM

entry.title: LPG business keeps Pryce January-March profit at P1B #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: MANILA, Philippines — Pryce Corp. posted a profit of P1.084 billion in the first quarter, driven by its strong liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) business. The January-March result was slightly higher than the P1.062 billion recorded in the same period last year. Pryce’s revenues rose by 3.39 percent to P5.98 billion from P5.79 billion. In a

entry.title: Gulf security model nears breaking point as external guarantees prove increasingly conditional #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: The Iran war of 2026 has revived long-standing warnings that Gulf monarchies may be entering a period of structural fragility, as reliance on external security guarantees is increasingly exposed as conditional and unreliable. Rather than imminent collapse, the region now faces pressure to either rapidly recalibrate into more autonomous and integrated defence arrangements or risk gradual political reconfiguration under intensifying external shocks.

entry.title: Indonesia commuter train crash toll rises to 15 as rescuers complete evacuation #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: The death toll from a train collision near the Indonesian capital Jakarta has risen to 15 with another 88 injured, a senior minister said on Tuesday, as emergency teams completed work to rescue passengers trapped in the wreckage.

entry.title: Coca-Cola tops estimates, raises earnings outlook as global beverage demand rises #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Shares of Coke have risen just 6% over the last year, hurt by concerns about the broader economy.

entry.title: Bank of Italy Urges Meloni Government to Keep Eye on Spending #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Italy needs to watch its spending if it wants to stick to European Union fiscal rules despite a worsening economic growth scenario, Bank of Italy official Andrea Brandolini said.

entry.title: As Las Vegas Strip Goes Quiet, Neighborhood Casinos Stay Busy #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: In Las Vegas, two gaming landscapes have emerged: the Strip is “dead,” as some doomsayers put it. Away from the neon lights and mega-resorts, locals are keeping neighborhood casinos humming.

entry.title: Here are 21 stocks to keep powering the tech rally, say these analysts #HowlStreet #Markets
entry.summary: Expect the push higher for tech stocks to keep going, says Barclays as Wall Street gets on board. They flag their favorite picks across tech sectors.
